title: Digital Wallet vs Bank Account
fullRewardHint: Answer all {{total}} choices correctly to earn the full reward.
reflectionPrompts: How do digital wallets and bank accounts serve different financial needs?
Why is it important to use the right tool for savings versus spending?

stages.prompt: Which is better for long-term savings?

options.label: Digital wallet
options.reflection: Digital wallets are primarily designed for transactions and everyday spending, not for long-term savings.

options.label: Both are equally good for savings
options.reflection: Bank accounts typically offer better interest rates, security features, and regulatory protection for savings.

options.label: Neither is good for savings
options.reflection: Bank accounts are designed to safely store money and often provide interest, making them suitable for savings.

options.label: Bank account
options.reflection: Exactly! Bank accounts offer better security, interest, and protection for long-term savings.

stages.prompt: What is a key difference between digital wallets and bank accounts?

options.label: Wallets are for spending; banks are for storing money safely
options.reflection: Exactly! Digital wallets are designed for convenience in transactions, while banks focus on secure storage and growth of money.

options.label: Banks use older technology than wallets
options.reflection: Both use modern technology, but their primary purposes differ significantly.

options.label: Banks always charge higher fees than wallets
options.reflection: Fee structures vary, but the main difference lies in the primary purpose of each service.

options.label: Banks are accessible 24/7 but wallets are not
options.reflection: Both digital wallets and online banking are typically accessible 24/7.

stages.prompt: Which typically offers better interest rates for savings?

options.label: Digital wallets
options.reflection: Digital wallets typically offer little to no interest on stored funds.

options.label: Both offer the same interest rates
options.reflection: Bank accounts typically offer significantly higher interest rates compared to digital wallets.

options.label: Neither offers interest on savings
options.reflection: Bank accounts typically offer interest to promote savings, while wallets generally do not.

options.label: Bank accounts
options.reflection: Exactly! Bank accounts, especially savings accounts, typically offer interest on deposits to encourage saving.

stages.prompt: Which provides better regulatory protection for your money?

options.label: Digital wallets
options.reflection: While wallets have some protection, banks are subject to more comprehensive regulatory oversight.

options.label: Both have equal regulatory protection
options.reflection: Banks typically have more comprehensive regulatory protection than digital wallets.

options.label: Bank accounts
options.reflection: Exactly! Bank accounts are heavily regulated with deposit insurance and consumer protection laws.

options.label: Neither provides regulatory protection
options.reflection: Bank accounts have substantial regulatory protection through deposit insurance and banking laws.

stages.prompt: For which purpose are digital wallets primarily designed?

options.label: Long-term savings
options.reflection: Digital wallets are not primarily designed for savings but for convenient transactions.

options.label: Convenient day-to-day transactions
options.reflection: Exactly! Digital wallets are designed for quick, convenient payments and money transfers.

options.label: Investing money
options.reflection: While some wallets offer investment features, their primary purpose is transaction convenience.

options.label: Providing financial insurance
options.reflection: Digital wallets are not primarily designed for insurance but for facilitating payments.
